summary: loadutils: typosquat of loader-utils with impersonated maintainer and unknown transitive dependency
details: Package name 'loadutils' is a close edit of the widely-used 'loader-utils' package. The README is copied from loader-utils documentation (urlToRequest, interpolateName, getHashDigest), but the actual code is a near-clone of 'debug-logfmt'. The package.json contributor block lists 'Kiko Beats' with the real maintainer's gmail address but substitutes the homepage URL with 'https://alphacointech1010.com', a crypto-themed domain unrelated to that maintainer — identity impersonation to lend false credibility. On require(), the package loads an undocumented dependency 'lessload' whose content is not analyzed here and which does not correspond to an established library. The two-layer impersonation (name mimics loader-utils, code/types mimic debug-logfmt with a substituted author URL) and the immediate pull-in of an unknown dependency on require() are consistent with a lure-and-dropper shape, but the harmful behavior, if any, lives in the 'lessload' transitive rather than in this package's own code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
